title: How to File a Charge of Employment Discrimination — EEOC
description: EEOC page on how private-sector workers file a charge of employment discrimination: online via the EEOC Public Portal, in person at a field office, by phone, at a state/local Fair Employment Practices Agency (FEPA), or by mail — including dual-filing with FEPAs and the time-limit rules.
notes: DEADLINE: 180 days from the act where no qualifying FEPA exists; 300 days where a state/local agency enforces a parallel law — Massachusetts qualifies via c.151B, so 300 days for most MA workers. Age-discrimination charges get 300 days only if a STATE (not merely local) agency enforces state age-discrimination law.
